kurti.explanation: 82% of returns in this category cite sizing issues due to mismatch between listing dimensions and actual garment.
Studio lighting and camera filters often cause high-saturation differences, prompting shade mismatch complaints.
Blends described as 'pure cotton' feel rough or synthetic to buyers, leading to texture-based returns.
Loose threads, uneven seams, or unhemmed linings cause complaints of cheap manufacturing.

saree.explanation: Inexpensive art silk or synthetic sarees are often stiff and uncomfortable to drape compared to product images.
Particularly visible on metallic gold/silver borders (zari) which look cheap or different in hand than in images.
Customers complain when the blouse piece is either not included, unseparated, or too small (less than 80cm).
Sequins or mirror work peeling off during unboxing or initial drape trials.

footwear.explanation: Indian feet tend to be wider, and standard European templates lead to immediate pinching/discomfort returns.
Flat shoes with zero heel support cause soreness, leading customers to flag them as unwearable.
Substandard glue line separation on first wear or during unboxing bend test.
Strong chemical or petrol smell from freshly packaged synthetic leather causes negative quality perception.

jewelry.explanation: Oxidation due to improper sealing/packaging moisture causes gold plating to look black or dull.
Weak solder points on link chains and cheap drawstrings break easily on first adjustment.
Heavy bridal sets or large earrings pull down earlobes, making them impractical for prolonged wear.
Glue failures lead to Kundan or beads dropping off inside the bubble wrap during transit.

home_decor.explanation: Fragile ceramic or glass items packaged without thick thermocol or bubble shells break easily.
Vases or planters appearing much smaller in real life than they do in mockup lifestyle listing photos.
Matte items looking glossy, or distressed paint finishes looking like defective/dirty spots.
Bumpy bottoms on tables or desks cause wobbly surfaces, making them unstable on flat floors.
